source.title: BSEE Accident Investigation Report - Structural Damage Crane/Other Lifting Top-Drive

context.region: Gulf of Mexico, Walker Ridge Block 758

actions_taken: Checklist developed for running riser operations
WIMS broken into stages with second crew member verification
Stop Work Authority Drill program to be developed by OIM
event.summary: On March 21, 2023 at 02:30 hours, a 25-pound riser bolt insert dislodged from a riser flange and fell approximately 75 feet to the rig floor on the drillship Valaris DS-18. The object passed between two Floorhands and struck an aluminum stairway, damaging two steps. The riser retainer pin was either never installed or fell out during transport. The retention cable (sling) required by WIMS Step 6, Item 'd' was not installed. Personnel failed to follow proper procedures and did not stop the job when the incident occurred.
recommendations: Develop checklist with all pertinent safety steps for running riser attached to WIMS
Break down long WIMS into stages with designated second crew member to verify compliance
Develop Stop Work Authority Drill program by OIM to increase personnel comfort in stopping jobs when needed

threats.name: Riser bolt insert dislodges from flange
Retainer pin not installed or lost during transport

controls.name: Work Instruction Management System (WIMS) - Riser Running Procedure
controls.barrier_role: Procedural control to ensure proper installation of retention devices
controls.lod_basis: Primary procedural control for safe riser operations

supporting_text: In Step 6, Item 'd' of the WIMS states, 'Confirm inserts are secured with sling.'
The sling is a retention cable secondary to the retaining pin which is weaved in and out of each riser bolt insert to secure them to the flange and was not installed at the time of the incident.
The On Tour Driller and Off Tour Driller did not review the entire WIMS and did not discuss Step 6, Item 'd,' with personnel.

controls.name: Riser retainer pin installation
controls.barrier_role: Primary mechanical retention device for riser bolt inserts
controls.lod_basis: Primary mechanical barrier to prevent bolt insert dislodgement

supporting_text: The riser retainer pin used to hold the riser bolt insert in place was either never installed or it fell out on the transport to the drill floor.

controls.name: Retention cable (sling) installation
controls.barrier_role: Secondary mechanical retention device for riser bolt inserts
controls.lod_basis: Secondary mechanical barrier weaved through bolt inserts

supporting_text: the sling is a retention cable secondary to the retaining pin which is weaved in and out of each riser bolt insert to secure them to the flange and was not installed at the time of the incident.
According to WIMS, the Subsea Engineer or other person assigned by the Sr. Subsea Engineer were to inspect the joint of riser to confirm all six bolt inserts were secured with a sling.

controls.name: Pre-tour safety meeting and WIMS review
controls.barrier_role: Communication and awareness control for safe work procedures
controls.lod_basis: Procedural control to brief personnel on hazards and requirements

supporting_text: On the morning of March 21, 2023, the 'A' drill crew held a pre-tour meeting, and the Driller discussed the topics of running riser with the Top Drive and good communication during the operation.
At 00:00 hours, the 'A' drill crew came on tour and held a safety meeting and reviewed the Work Instruction Management System (WIMS) on running Blow Out Preventer (BOP) on marine riser.
The On Tour Driller and Off Tour Driller did not review the entire WIMS and did not discuss Step 6, Item 'd,' with personnel.

controls.name: Subsea Engineer inspection of riser joint
controls.barrier_role: Verification control to confirm retention devices are properly installed
controls.lod_basis: Independent verification of critical safety requirements

supporting_text: According to WIMS, the Subsea Engineer or other person assigned by the Sr. Subsea Engineer were to inspect the joint of riser to confirm all six bolt inserts were secured with a sling.

controls.name: Stop work authority and incident reporting
controls.barrier_role: Immediate response control to halt operations and escalate hazards
controls.lod_basis: Emergency response to prevent escalation of incident consequences

supporting_text: Neither Floorhand stopped the job, and they continued to make up the riser bolts on the spider.
It took several minutes before one of the Floorhands realized they were nearly struck by a 25 pound riser bolt insert
It was then reported to the Assistant Driller (AD), who notified the Driller, who then informed the Toolpusher (TP)

notes.rules: JSON output only. mentioned fields must be evidence-based. Use null for unknown values.
